learningObjectives: Use core services vocabulary at A2 level.
Use can, could, must and have to in everyday service situations.
Choose formal or informal language appropriately.
Match people to short service texts.
Complete notes from short service announcements.
Write a formal message about a service problem.
Answer interview questions about using local services.
grammarFocus: Modals: can, could, must, have to

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I opened a bank account at the city branch.
The postage for this letter is one pound twenty.
Please wait at reception until your name is called.

vocabularyBank.vietnameseMeaning: câu hỏi / yêu cầu thông tin
vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I made an enquiry about my bank charges.
My appointment with the doctor is at ten thirty.
She wrote a complaint about the long queue.
Keep the receipt if you want a refund.
Registration for new students opens on Monday.
Pay for the postage at the counter.
Show your receipt to get a refund.
Complete the registration form at reception.
There was a long queue at the post office.

grammarReference.structure: Modals: can, could, must, have to
grammarReference.explanation: Use can/could for ability and polite requests. Use must/have to for rules and obligations. Have to often describes external rules; must can sound stronger or more personal.
examples: Could I make an appointment, please?
You must bring your registration card.
You have to complete the form at the counter.
Can you help me at reception?
commonMistakes: You must to bring your ID (×) → You must bring your ID (✓)
I have bring the receipt (×) → I have to bring the receipt (✓)
Could I to ask a question? (×) → Could I ask a question? (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Formal language uses polite phrases (Could you..., I would like..., Dear Sir/Madam). Informal language uses short forms and friendly phrases (Can you..., Hi, Thanks). Choose register to match the situation.
examples: Formal: I would like to make an enquiry about my bank account.
Informal: Can you check my account, please?
Formal: I am writing to complain about the service.
Informal: I'm not happy with the queue today.
commonMistakes: Hey Sir, give me a refund (×) → Dear Sir, I would like a refund (✓)
I am writing to complain lol (×) → I am writing to complain about the delay (✓)
Could you kindly stuff (×) → Could you kindly help me? (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Services Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve words for banks, post offices and other everyday services at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Services Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: Money you keep in a bank is in your _____.
questions.explanation: Bank account (tài khoản ngân hàng) là nơi lưu tiền. Receipt là biên lai.

questions.questionText: The price you pay to send a letter is called _____.
questions.explanation: Postage (cước phí bưu chính) là phí gửi thư. Queue là hàng đợi.

questions.questionText: When you book a time to see a doctor, you have an _____.
questions.explanation: Appointment (cuộc hẹn) là thời gian đã đặt trước. Complaint là khiếu nại.

questions.questionText: A paper that shows you paid is a _____.
questions.explanation: Receipt (biên lai) chứng minh bạn đã thanh toán. Form là mẫu đơn.

questions.questionText: Signing up for a service or course is called _____.
questions.explanation: Registration (đăng ký) là ghi danh. Enquiry là hỏi thông tin.

questions.questionText: People waiting in a line form a _____.
questions.explanation: Queue (hàng đợi) là dãy người chờ. Counter là quầy giao dịch.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the services words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với một nghĩa rõ ràng. Reception và counter thường bị nhầm.

pairs.right: the desk where visitors first ask for help
a question or request for information
when you say you are unhappy with a service
money returned when something is wrong

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: You pay for stamps and letters at the post office _____.
questions.explanation: Counter (quầy giao dịch) là nơi trả tiền. Appointment là cuộc hẹn.

questions.questionText: If the service was bad, you might make a _____.
questions.explanation: Complaint (khiếu nại) khi dịch vụ không tốt. Postage là phí gửi thư.

questions.questionText: Before you open a bank account, you often fill in a _____.
questions.explanation: Form (mẫu đơn) cần điền khi mở tài khoản. Queue là hàng đợi.

questions.questionText: Wait at _____ until the clerk calls your name.
questions.explanation: Reception (lễ tân) là nơi chờ và hỏi thông tin. Refund là hoàn tiền.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: I kept the [0] after paying the [1] at the post office.
questions.explanation: receipt (biên lai) sau khi trả postage (cước phí).

content.template: I kept the [0] after paying the [1] at the post office.

questions.questionText: Complete: She made an [0] about the long [1] at reception.
questions.explanation: enquiry (hỏi thông tin) hoặc complaint — ở đây complaint về queue.

content.template: She made a [0] about the long [1] at reception.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Modals and Register
lessons.learningObjective: Use can, could, must and have to correctly, and choose formal or informal language in service contexts.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Modal
exercises.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: _____ I make an appointment, please?
questions.explanation: Could + I — yêu cầu lịch sự ở quầy dịch vụ.

choices.distractorNote: Too strong for a polite request

questions.questionText: You _____ bring your registration card to every visit.
questions.explanation: Must/have to — quy định bắt buộc. Must phù hợp với quy tắc y tế.

questions.questionText: To open a bank account, you _____ visit the branch with your ID.
questions.explanation: Have to — quy định bên ngoài (ngân hàng yêu cầu).

questions.questionText: Which sentence is most formal?
questions.explanation: I would like to make an enquiry — cấu trúc trang trọng cho email/thư.

choices.text: I would like to make an enquiry about my receipt.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: You [0] to show your receipt for a refund. | [1] I speak to someone at reception?
questions.explanation: have to (bắt buộc); Could (yêu cầu lịch sự).

content.template: You [0] to show your receipt for a refund. [1] I speak to someone at reception?

questions.questionText: Complete: Visitors [0] wait in the queue. | You [1] complete the registration form online.
questions.explanation: must (quy tắc); can (khả năng/permission).

content.template: Visitors [0] wait in the queue. You [1] complete the registration form online.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Dear Sir, I am writing to _____ about the long queue yesterday.
questions.explanation: complain — động từ trang trọng trong thư khiếu nại.

choices.distractorNote: Gerund less natural after to here

questions.questionText: You _____ smoke in the medical centre reception area.
questions.explanation: must not / can't — cấm. Must alone means obligation.

questions.questionText: _____ you help me with this registration form?
questions.explanation: Could — lịch sự hơn Can trong tình huống trang trọng.

questions.questionText: Which reply is appropriate at a bank counter?
questions.explanation: I would like to open a bank account, please — formal và lịch sự.

choices.text: I would like to open a bank account, please.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: You / have to / bring / your receipt / for a refund.
questions.explanation: You have to bring your receipt for a refund. — have to + động từ nguyên mẫu.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: Could / I / make / an appointment / at reception?
questions.explanation: Could I make an appointment at reception? — Could + chủ ngữ + động từ nguyên mẫu.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / would like / to make / an enquiry / about my bank account.
questions.explanation: I would like to make an enquiry about my bank account. — register trang trọng.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Service Messages
lessons.learningObjective: Match people to short service texts and find details about rules, appointments and registration.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Messages
exercises.instructions: Read the four messages. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Which Service Do You Need?
passage.text:
CITY HELP DESK — FOUR SHORT MESSAGES

Message A — Bank
Need a new bank account? Visit the counter on Floor 2 with your ID and proof of address. You must complete a registration form before the bank can open your account. Appointments are available on weekdays.

Message B — Post Office
Standard postage for a small letter is £1.20. Keep your receipt if you send something valuable. You can buy stamps at the counter or use the self-service machine near reception.

Message C — Medical Centre
For a routine appointment, call reception before 4 p.m. If you have a complaint about waiting times, speak to the manager at the enquiry desk. You must bring your registration card to every visit.

Message D — Council Office
Lost your parking permit? Make an enquiry online or at the help desk. You have to pay a small fee and show your receipt from the last payment. Registration for new residents opens every Monday morning.
passage.imagePrompt: Four short official notices on a help desk board about bank, post office, medical centre and council services; clean A2 infographic style.

questions.questionText: Where must you go to open a bank account?
questions.explanation: Message A: Visit the counter on Floor 2 with your ID.

questions.questionText: What must you bring to every medical visit?
questions.explanation: Message C: You must bring your registration card to every visit.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Messages
exercises.instructions: Read the messages again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Which Service Do You Need?
passage.text:

Message B — Post Office
Standard postage for a small letter is £1.20. Keep your receipt if you send something valuable. You can buy stamps at the counter or use the self-service machine near reception.

Message C — Medical Centre
For a routine appointment, call reception before 4 p.m. If you have a complaint about waiting times, speak to the manager at the enquiry desk. You must bring your registration card to every visit.

Message D — Council Office
Lost your parking permit? Make an enquiry online or at the help desk. You have to pay a small fee and show your receipt from the last payment. Registration for new residents opens every Monday morning.
passage.imagePrompt: Four short official notices on a help desk board about bank, post office, medical centre and council services; clean A2 infographic style.

questions.questionText: In Message B, why should you keep your receipt?
questions.explanation: Keep your receipt if you send something valuable — bảo hiểm/chứng minh gửi.

choices.text: To register as a new resident

questions.questionText: Why might someone go to the enquiry desk in Message D?
questions.explanation: Lost parking permit — make an enquiry online or at the help desk.

choices.text: They need postage for a letter
They want a new bank account

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the messages one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: Which Service Do You Need?

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of all four messages?
questions.explanation: Mỗi tin nhắn hướng dẫn người dùng dịch vụ công cộng phải làm gì.

choices.text: To tell people what to do at different services

questions.questionText: Put the steps for opening a bank account in order: (1) Complete form (2) Visit counter (3) Bring ID
questions.explanation: Message A: visit with ID → complete registration form → account opened.

choices.distractorNote: ID is needed when you visit

exercises.title: Apply: Match People to Messages
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each person to the correct message (A–D).

questions.questionText: Match each person to the message they should read.
questions.explanation: Mỗi người khớp với dịch vụ phù hợp trong bốn tin nhắn.

pairs.left: Hoa has a complaint about waiting

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Service Calls
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to service calls and notes to identify appointments, rules and enquiry details.

exercises.title: Learn: Making an Appointment
exercises.instructions: Listen to the call. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: Making a Doctor's Appointment

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Good morning. City Medical Centre, reception. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Yes, we can book an appointment for Thursday at ten thirty. Could you spell your surname, please?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Thank you. You must bring your registration card and any recent receipts for medicine.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: If you need to change the time, call reception before three o'clock. Have a good day.

script.audioNotes: Friendly professional tone, moderate pace, clear phone/reception ambience. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: When is the appointment?
questions.explanation: We can book an appointment for Thursday at ten thirty.

questions.questionText: What must the caller bring?
questions.explanation: You must bring your registration card and any recent receipts.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Appointment Call
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same call. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Making a Doctor's Appointment

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Good morning. City Medical Centre, reception. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Yes, we can book an appointment for Thursday at ten thirty. Could you spell your surname, please?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Thank you. You must bring your registration card and any recent receipts for medicine.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: If you need to change the time, call reception before three o'clock. Have a good day.

script.audioNotes: Friendly professional tone, moderate pace, clear phone/reception ambience. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: When must you call if you need to change the time?
questions.explanation: Call reception before three o'clock.

questions.questionText: What does the receptionist ask the caller to do?
questions.explanation: Could you spell your surname, please?

exercises.title: Check: Bank Enquiry Call
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new call. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Bank Enquiry Call
script.setting: Phone call to a high street bank

lines.speaker: Bank clerk
lines.text: Hello, Riverside Bank. This is customer services. How may I help you today?

lines.speaker: Bank clerk
lines.text: To open a bank account, you have to visit a branch with your ID. You could also start registration online first.

lines.speaker: Bank clerk
lines.text: If you have a complaint about a charge, please keep your receipt and we will check it.

lines.speaker: Bank clerk
lines.text: You can speak to someone at the enquiry desk on Monday from nine to five. Thank you for calling.

script.audioNotes: Formal telephone voice, steady pace, light office background. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: What do you have to do to open a bank account?
questions.explanation: You have to visit a branch with your ID.

choices.text: Visit a branch with your ID

questions.questionText: What should you keep if you have a complaint about a charge?
questions.explanation: Please keep your receipt and we will check it.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Calls
exercises.instructions: Listen to the appointment call again. Match each topic to the correct fact.

questions.questionText: Match each topic to a fact from the appointment call.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với thông tin trong cuộc gọi đặt lịch.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Write About a Service Problem
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short formal message about a service problem using modals and services vocabulary.

exercises.title: Learn: Formal Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: Dear, complaint, receipt, must, thank.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] Sir, | I am writing to make a [1] about my visit. | I [2] attach my [3].
questions.explanation: Dear; complaint; must (hoặc will); receipt — khung thư trang trọng.

content.template:
[0] Sir,

I am writing to make a [1] about my visit. I [2] attach my [3].

exercises.title: Practice: Build Formal Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / would like / to make / an enquiry / about my appointment.
questions.explanation: I would like to make an enquiry about my appointment.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: You / have to / wait / in the queue / at reception.
questions.explanation: You have to wait in the queue at reception.

exercises.title: Check: Message About a Service Problem
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a service manager. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: You visited the post office yesterday. You paid the wrong postage and waited in a long queue. Write a formal message to the manager.
prompts: Explain the problem with the postage or queue.
Say what you would like (for example a refund or help).
successCriteria: Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Dear Manager,

I am writing to make a complaint about my visit yesterday. I paid the wrong postage at the counter and I had to wait in a long queue. I have my receipt and I would like a refund, please. Could you help me with this enquiry?

Thank you,
Minh

grammar.criteria: Uses can/could/must/have to or formal phrases correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses services words (complaint, receipt, queue, postage) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, body and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Explains the problem and says what you want; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Reception, | I [0] to change my [1]. | I can come on Thursday. | [2] you, | Lan
questions.explanation: need (need to); appointment; Thank — email lịch sự.

content.template:
Dear Reception,

I [0] to change my [1]. I can come on Thursday.

[2] you,
Lan

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Using Services
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about using banks, post offices and other services.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Where do you pay for postage? You say:
questions.explanation: At the counter — trả lời ngắn, đúng từ vựng A2.

choices.text: At the counter in the post office.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What do you need for a doctor's appointment? You say:
questions.explanation: Registration card — thẻ đăng ký thường cần khi khám.

choices.text: I must bring my registration card.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the most appropriate reply in each service situation.

questions.questionText: The queue is very long. You want to complain politely. You say:
questions.explanation: Excuse me, I would like to make a complaint — lịch sự, formal.

choices.text: Excuse me, I would like to make a complaint about the queue.

questions.questionText: You need information at reception. You say:
questions.explanation: Could I make an enquiry...? — modal lịch sự.

choices.text: Could I make an enquiry about my appointment, please?

exercises.title: Check: Interview About Services
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions. Record your answers.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: You lost your receipt but want a refund. You say:
questions.explanation: Could you help me without a receipt? — lịch sự, thực tế.

choices.text: Could you help me? I am afraid I lost my receipt.
You must give refund no receipt.

questions.questionText: You want to register for a new library card at reception. You say:
questions.explanation: I would like to register, please — formal and clear.

choices.text: I would like to register for a library card, please.
